Title: Heineken Malaysia's Q1 net profit falls 14pct to RM104mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Heineken Malaysia Bhd's net profit fell 14 per cent to RM104 million in the first quarter of financial year 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM122 million due to softer consumer sentiment.
The company's performance was also affected by a deliberate and proactive reduction in the company's ex-brewery sales to align with challenging market dynamics, laying the foundation for stronger underlying momentum going forward.
Heineken Malaysia's revenue dropped 13 per cent to RM664 million from RM763.6 million, according to a filing to Bursa Malaysia.
Managing director Martijn van Keulen said the quarterly performance reflected a more challenging operating environment, with softer consumer sentiment and geopolitical developments influencing spending patterns.
"We continue to be firmly focused on a targeted consumer-led approach to strengthen our growth fundamentals and further improving our productivity through revenue optimisation and structural cost efficiency.
"While external conditions remain evolving, these actions strengthen our resilience and position us to capture opportunities as market conditions improve, supporting long-term value creation under our EverGreen 2030 strategy," he said in a statement.
Under its EverGreen 2030 strategy, Martijn said technology and agility remain central to long-term growth and productivity.
The company's Digital Backbone programme - one of its largest transformation initiatives - aims to strengthen its digital foundation by standardising processes, harmonising end-to-end operations and enhancing data utilisation across the business.
The rollout, which integrates multiple digital platforms to improve automation, consistency and decision-making, is on track for completion by Q4 2026.
The company acommended government and enforcement agencies for continued efforts to combat illicit beer trade, which it said remains a major challenge for the industry.
It noted that the beer sector contributes RM7.1 billion annually to the economy, RM3.3 billion in tax revenue, and supports more than 52,000 jobs, based on the latest Confederation of Malaysian Brewers Bhd's Economic Impact Assessment.
However, it estimated that illicit beer accounts for about 25 per cent of the market, resulting in around RM1.2 billion in lost annual tax revenue.
On the outlook, Martijn said the market remains soft amid persistent geopolitical uncertainty, including tensions in the Middle East, alongside subdued consumer sentiment.
These factors are expected to continue weighing on demand and cost pressures, reinforcing the need for disciplined execution and operational agility.
Following the announcement on the transition of Asia Pacific Breweries (Singapore) Pte Ltd to an import-based supply model, exports from the group are expected to begin in Q3 2026.
This supports its EverGreen 2030 strategy to optimise supply chain capacity, improve economies of scale and enhance operational efficiency.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang's river sand export issue raised during MB's meeting with minister in Putrajaya
Article:
KUANTAN: Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail today paid a courtesy call on Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup.
The meeting, held at Arthur's office in Putrajaya around noon today, is believed to have touched on matters related to river sand exports in the east coast state.
During the Pahang assembly sitting last week, Wan Rosdy said he would be meeting Arthur
to discuss efforts
to revive river sand exports in a bid to boost state revenue.
In a Facebook post, Wan Rosdy described the meeting today as an opportunity to exchange views and discuss various matters related to natural resource management and environmental sustainability.
"Efforts to strengthen cooperation between the federal and Pahang governments for the well-being of the people and sustainable development were also raised.
"May all the discussions bring benefits to the state and the people as a whole," he wrote.
The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, had previously
expressed disappointment
over the federal government's move to ban river sand exports, prompting Wan Rosdy to meet the ministry to discuss the matter in greater detail.
The New Straits Times has reached out to Wan Rosdy's office on whether they will issue a statement on the meeting and its outcome, particularly on the issue of river sand exports.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't use AI to 'beautify' passport photos, Immigration warns
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Immigration Department has issued a stern warning to passport applicants against using Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology to "beautify" their application photos following the detection of several such cases recently.
Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said while the department encouraged the use of online renewal services, some applicants had been caught submitting digitally manipulated images to improve their appearance.
"We continue to encourage the public to apply online as it is our standard procedure. However, I must stress that applicants must not submit photos generated or enhanced by AI technology," Zakaria said at a press conference today.
He said the trend appeared to be driven by vanity, with applicants attempting to alter their physical features or skin tones.
"We have detected several cases where AI was used. It seems some want a 'prettier' photo — perhaps changing a darker complexion to a fairer one, or trying to look more 'handsome' than they are.
"At first glance, the photo may look like the applicant, but our processing officers have the expertise to differentiate between a genuine photograph and one that has been manipulated," he said.
Zakaria warned that those attempting to use AI-enhanced photos would ultimately be detected during the final stage of the process.
"Even if they think they can bypass the system initially, we will detect the discrepancy when they come to the counter for collection. At that point, they will be flagged," he said.
While he did not provide an exact figure, Zakaria confirmed that "more than three" such cases had already been identified.
He reiterated that the public should continue using online application platforms, provided they strictly adhered to the department's official photograph guidelines.
Zakaria spoke to reporters after the Immigration Department honoured 364 Excellent Service Award recipients and five recipients of the Pingat Setia Bakti at its 2025 Excellence Service Award ceremony today.
A total of 1,212 personnel nationwide have been selected for the awards, which will be held regionally.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends firmer vs major currencies, eases against greenback
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended mostly higher against major currencies but eased against the US dollar on Tuesday as market participants await further developments in Middle East negotiations between the United States (US) and Iran.
At 6pm, the ringgit edged down to 3.9745/9785 versus the greenback from 3.9720/9770 at Monday's close.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said market participants are awaiting further developments on the war in Iran as negotiations between the US and Iran continue.
"Meanwhile, WTI and Brent crude prices fell 0.15 per cent and 1.51 per cent to US$108.50 per barrel and US$110.41 per barrel, respectively, as hopes for a resolution to the war strengthened," he told Bernama
Meanwhile, earlier today, the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said Malaysia's inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), rose 1.9 per cent year-on-year in April 2026 to 136.9 from 134.3 in the same month last year.
DOSM said Malaysia's April inflation was lower than that of several countries, including the Philippines, Vietnam, Thailand, the Republic of Korea and Indonesia.
At the close, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
It strengthened versus the Japanese yen to 2.4980/5006 from 2.4991/5024 and appreciated against the euro to 4.6180/6226 from 4.6214/6272 at yesterday's close, but eased against the British pound to 5.3282/3336 from 5.3078/3145 previously.
At the same time, the local currency traded mixed against regional peers, rising against the Singapore dollar to 3.1022/1055 from 3.1036/1078 at Monday's close, and strengthening against the Indonesian rupiah to 224.4/224.8 from 224.8/225.1 yesterday.
However, it eased against the Thai baht to 12.1719/1898 from 12.1646/1848, and was flat against the Philippine peso at 6.43/6.45 on Monday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ronnie Liu: Selangor ruler's message goes beyond Dataran Selangor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah's decree on the importance of understanding the Rukun Negara serves as a reminder for politicians to return to the fundamental principles of nation-building, says former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu.
Liu said the ruler's message was not merely about attending Dataran Selangor, but about urging politicians to understand the meaning of respect for the nation, the Constitution, the rule of law, the institution of the Rulers and Malaysians.
He said the five pillars of the Rukun Negara should not be understood selectively, but fully internalised as the foundation of Malaysia's political culture.
"I, with humility and highest appreciation, would like to express my gratitude to His Highness the Sultan of Selangor for reminding all politicians to once again understand the true spirit of the Rukun Negara, especially in a political landscape and society increasingly filled with emotion and division.
"In my view, His Highness' decree for politicians to attend Dataran Selangor twice a month to be closer to the rakyat and to appreciate the Rukun Negara is not merely symbolic or for formality.
"But it carries a much deeper meaning, which is a reminder for all politicians to return to the fundamental principles of nation-building," he said in a statement today.
Liu added that as someone long involved in politics and democratic struggles, he deeply valued the supremacy of the Constitution as the cornerstone of Malaysia's stability and harmony.
He said that in recognition of this principle, he named his child "Zun Xian", which means respecting the Constitution, symbolising that future generations must also be built upon respect for the law, Constitution and the nation's foundational principles.
"New-era politics should no longer be built on hatred, emotion and division, but must instead be built on maturity, rationality, respect and the nation's future.
"What the people expect today is not for politicians to continue quarrelling, but for them to return to national principles and work together to build a Malaysia that is more mature, stable, united and full of hope for future generations," he said.
This came after Sultan Sharafuddin told Liu and Seri Kembangan assemblyman Wong Siew Ki
to read the Rukun Negara every week
to better understand its contents and become courteous citizens who respect the royal institution.
The ruler also said all assemblymen in the state should go to Dataran Selangor twice a month to read the Rukun Negara inscribed on a plaque at the square.
Previously, Wong proposed in the state assembly allowing modern closed-system pig farming in Selangor in response to a royal decree in February in which the Sultan declined to consent to the practice in any district in the state.
Meanwhile, Liu had defended Wong's proposal as a technical solution raised within a lawful institutional framework.
It was also reported that Liu had urged pig farmers in Selangor to
consider filing for judicial review,
reiterating that the Federal Constitution safeguards their rights.
This followed
Sultan Sharafuddin's reiteration
that he did not consent to pig farming activities in the state, in line with his royal decree.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 19,700 undocumented migrants deported this year amid intensified crackdown
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A total of 19,700 undocumented migrants have been deported to their home countries so far this year, as the Immigration Department intensifies its crackdown on illegal immigration.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said Indonesians made up the largest group of the deportees with 7,936 individuals.
"Indonesians lead the list of detainees at 7,936, followed by Myanmar (4,005), Bangladesh (2,453), the Philippines (1,325), and Thailand (1,008).
"These five nations represent the highest cohorts. The remaining detainees originate from various other countries, with some groups consisting of only a handful of individuals," he told a press conference here today.
Currently, over 22,000 detainees are being held across 20 immigration depots nationwide, including several temporary facilities.
These figures come on the back of a massive enforcement drive. Since Jan 1, the department has conducted 4,785 operations nationwide, inspecting 69,406 individuals.
These raids resulted in the detention of 16,681 immigrants for various offences, as well as the arrest of 730 employers suspected of hiring or sheltering undocumented workers.
"For this year, we have significantly intensified our enforcement operations to be even more aggressive. We will not compromise on any violation of the Immigration Act," Zakaria said.
In addition to forced deportations, Zakaria highlighted the success of the Migrant Repatriation Program 2.0, which encourages undocumented migrants to surrender voluntarily.
Last year, the program saw 257,443 migrants from 112 countries register to return home, with the government collecting RM128,726,940 in compounds.
Indonesians again made up the bulk of voluntary surrenders at 111,660, followed by Bangladeshis (49,481) and Myanmar nationals (33,858).
Zakaria explained that voluntary repatriation is a more pragmatic and "cost-effective" approach compared to forced enforcement.
"Enforcement operations involve a much higher cost to the government. The PRM provides an opportunity for migrants to surrender, pay a lower compound, and return home safely.
"However, they are not being pardoned. Every participant is blacklisted and barred from re-entering Malaysia.
"This approach not only saves taxpayers' money but also generates revenue for the state," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 19, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended marginally lower as investors turned more defensive amid escalating geopolitical tensions and weaker regional risk sentiment, with selling pressure focused on selected index heavyweights.
The ringgit continued to weaken for a fourth consecutive day, trading at 3.9730 against the US dollar.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin rose to RM306,517.
Ethereum and Solana also traded higher at RM8,468 and RM338.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dutch Lady Q1 earnings rises 20pct to RM29.96mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd's net profit rose 19.7 per cent to RM29.96 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM25.03 million in the same period last year.
The company said improvement in earnings was due to strong volume performance, the absence of one-off transition-related costs, lower commodity costs, positive exchange rate developments and revenue growth management initiatives.
These were partially offset by inventory revaluation and investment in advertising and promotion to strengthen brand equity and support sales growth.
Revenue for the quarter under review rose 6.5 per cent to RM397.84 million versus RM373.4 million a year ago due to higher sales in its liquid milk range, strong momentum in the out-of-home channel and continued growth from new products launched in 2025.
"The strong sales performance demonstrates the resilience of our core portfolio, sustained momentum from product innovations and successful acquisitions of new accounts in the out-of-home channel. Market conditions remained supportive, boosted by a strong Ramadan season and the Sumbangan Asas Rahmah assistance,"  it said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia.
Moving forward, the company said that the Middle East war has significantly disrupted supply and demand balances, and puts upward pressure on the costs of energy, shipping and raw materials.
"The company has not faced any significant raw material shortages yet, but foresees input costs to significantly increase, especially in the second half of 2026," it added.
It added that dairy raw material prices have been relatively stable in Q1 FY26, but are also expected to increase in the remainder of the year, exerting pressure on production costs and margins.
In addition, ongoing inflation of fuel prices increases costs of energy, services and logistics.
"Consumer demand for essential nutrition products is expected to remain resilient, yet increasing inflation and uncertainty caused by the blocking of the Strait of Hormuz, affects consumer confidence and spending power, which could have an impact on future demand for consumer goods."
The company said that it will continue its stringent management of raw material supply to ensure availability of its products to Malaysian consumers.
It will also respond to the imminent cost inflation with a double-down focus on cost management.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Automotive industry's TIV rises 14pct to 72,113 units in April
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's automotive industry recorded a 14 per cent increase in total industry volume (TIV) to 72,113 vehicles in April 2026 compared to the preceding month.
The Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) said the number reflects two consecutive months of growth.
"The higher TIV in April 2026 was mainly supported by higher production volume of 70, 611 units in April 2026 after the Raya plant shutdown in March 2026 and sales from new model launches," it said in a statement today.
Production rose 25 per cent to 70, 611 units in April 2026 from 56,602 units a year earlier.
Meanwhile, for the January-April 2026 period, sales inched up two per cent to 254,318 units compared with 250,239 units in the corresponding four-month period last year.
Production fell two per cent to 229,988 units from 234,205 units in the same quarter of 2025.
Regarding May's outlook, MAA anticipates sales to maintain the current momentum.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Number of M'sians detained by Israeli regime rises to 25
Article:
SEPANG: The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) confirmed that 25 Malaysians who were on 15 ships of Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 were among those detained after dozens of humanitarian aid ships were reportedly intercepted by the Israeli navy (IOF) while en route to Gaza.
SNCC director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said a total of 40 flotilla ships involving nearly 350 humanitarian activists had been intercepted and raided as of 3pm yesterday (May 19).
He said SNCC had yet to receive confirmation on the status and location of all the activists who were detained, including whether they were still at sea or had been taken to Ashdod port.
"SNCC received visual reports confirming that all activists were forcibly abducted from their respective ships onto four regime military vessels.
"To date, SNCC has yet to receive confirmation on the status of all detained activists, including the location and destination of their transfer after the interception incident," he said in a press conference here today.
Sani Araby said SNCC was closely monitoring two possible locations for the transfer of humanitarian aid flotilla activists detained by the Israeli regime, either heading to Greece or the port of Ashdod.
He said the first scenario was based on initial information claiming that the Israeli military ship carrying the activists was moving towards Greece while the second scenario was based on the announcement by Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani that the ships were expected to arrive at the port of Ashdod tomorrow.
According to him, both possibilities had a high probability, but until now the exact location of all detained activists had not been ascertained.
"It is possible that Israel is trying to divert attention and until now we have not yet been certain where they will actually be taken," he said.
Sani Araby said the ships that have been intercepted and detained by IOF are Cactus, Marilyn, Jandabar, Holy Blue, Sadabad, Zio Faster, Blue Toys, Kyriakos, Josef, Barbaros, Tenaz, Ozgurluk, Isobella, Amanda, Umut, Furleto, Boralize, Vincovolo, Amazon, Bianca ITA, Abodes, Gotico, Bye Luby, Themis, La Cirena, Last Dream, Adalah, Rim, Peluxo, Iridescence, Belella, Atlantic Blue, Venus, Isias, Azur, Nablus, Diabolo, L'Arq, Perseverance and Munki.
He said the Malaysians involved in the incident were the Azur ship carrying Razman Mat Ali, Nor Liyana Farhan Daud (Amazon), Al Ahmad Shah Abdul Kadir (Iridescence) and the Marilyn ship carrying Abdul Rahim Abdul Razak and Eddy Noor Mad Amin.
Also arrested was the Bolarize ship carrying Mohd Hanafi Mohd Salim and Mohd Redzal Amzah, as well as the Ozgurluk ship carrying Jihan Alya Mohd Nordin and Norhelmi Ab Ghani.
Yesterday, 16 Malaysians were arrested, including Zainal Rashid Ahmad (on board the Amanda ship), Muhamad Muhsin Zaidi (Isobella) and Mohammad Shazly Mohammad Shees and Tengku Ahmad Taufik Tengku Noor Zahari (Cactus).
Also arrested were S. Patmanathan, Muhammad Amirul Afif Mohd Azhar and Dr Syakirin Muhamed Senin (Ferluto), Abdul Samad Selamat and Naufa Azhari (Bianca ITA), Mohammad Farizzal Rostam and Muhammad Zaid Abdul Karim (Gotico) and Norazman Ishak (Abodes).
In addition, also arrested were Hazwan Hazim Dermawan, Mohd Shamsir Mohd Isa and Osman Zolkifli (Themis) and Ahmad Musa Al-Nuwayri Kamaruzaman (Josef).
Sani Araby said SNCC also confirmed that several delegations from other countries operated by Sumud Nusantara were also arrested, involving activists from Pakistan, Sri Lanka and Indonesia, namely Abdul Sattar (Zio Faster ship), Sameera (Umut ), Thoudy Badai, Heru Rahendro and Andre Prasetyo (Ozgurluk), as well as Bambang Noroyono (Bolarize) and Andi Anga Prasadewa (Josef).
"SNCC condemns this interception and kidnapping as a pirate act, a despicable act and a clear violation of international law, particularly the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (Unclos) and a serious violation of the principle of freedom of humanitarian missions carried out in international waters," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends marginally lower as investors turn defensive
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended marginally lower on Tuesday as investors adopted a more defensive stance amid escalating geopolitical tensions and softer regional risk sentiment.
At 5pm, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) edged down 0.44 of a point, or 0.03 per cent, to 1,727.27 from yesterday's close of 1,727.71.
The benchmark index, which opened 5.17 points higher at 1,732.88, moved between 1,726.13 and 1,734.32 throughout the day.
Market breadth was negative with losers leading gainers 618 to 509, while 610 counters were unchanged, 967 untraded and 15 suspended.
Turnover was lower at 3.36 billion units worth RM3.24 billion compared with 3.51 billion units worth RM2.84 billion on Monday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the FBM KLCI was also weighed down by selling pressure concentrated in selected index heavyweights.
"Sentiment was further tempered by Malaysia's April headline inflation accelerating to 1.9 per cent year-on-year from 1.7 per cent previously, reinforcing concerns surrounding lingering cost pressures and the potential moderation in consumer demand momentum.
"Nevertheless, downside was partially offset by continued strength in the retail segment, where several counters rallied following stronger-than-expected first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026) earnings, underpinned by resilient spending trends and margin expansion," he told Bernama.
Meanwhile, he said foreign investors remained net sellers for the fifth consecutive session as of yesterday, highlighting persistent external caution and ongoing portfolio reallocation amid elevated global macro and geopolitical uncertainties.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said market sentiment is still being capped by elevated crude oil prices, unresolved geopolitical tensions in Middle East, uncertainty over US interest rates and concerns over global inflation.
"While the easing oil prices may provide some near-term relief to market sentiment, investors are likely to remain cautious and selective until there is greater clarity on external developments," he said.
He expects the benchmark index to remain range-bound in the near term as bargain-hunting activities may gradually emerge on market weakness, while upside momentum could remain limited by the cautious external environment.
"We anticipate the benchmark index to trend within the range of 1,720-1,750 for the rest of the week," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirAsia flight turns back to KLIA after technical issue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An AirAsia flight bound for Sandakan was forced to turn back to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) after experiencing a technical issue shortly after takeoff on Sunday.
The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM), when contacted by the New Straits Times, confirmed the matter.
"AirAsia flight AK5192 requested to return to Kuala Lumpur after the pilot detected a technical issue during the flight. The aircraft later landed safely," it said.
According to flight tracking website Flightradar24, the Airbus A320 departed KLIA at 6.24am before turning back over Negri Sembilan about 12 minutes later.
The aircraft then flew over the Strait of Malacca in a holding pattern before landing safely at KLIA at 7.17am.
The New Straits Times has reached out to AirAsia for further details on the technical issue.
Meanwhile, a social media posting believed to be from a passenger on board claimed the aircraft's right engine appeared to malfunction and emit flames, sparking panic among passengers.
The post further alleged that the aircraft circled in the air before making a safe landing at KLIA, where emergency services were reportedly on standby.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New M'sian passport to roll out June 1 will boast 94 security features
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Immigration Department will begin issuing the next generation of the Malaysian International Passport on June 1, featuring nearly double the security features of the current version to thwart forgery.
Immigration Director-General Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the enhanced document will boast 94 security features, a significant upgrade from the 49 features found in the existing passport.
"The new Malaysian International Passport will be issued starting June 1, 2026.
"While the current version is already of high quality, the latest iteration features significantly enhanced security specifications.
"We have increased the security features from 49 to 94. This nearly twofold increase is aimed at making the document more secure and significantly harder to counterfeit," Zakaria said after the Immigration Department's 2025 Excellence Service Awards.
He explained that the upgrades involve sophisticated technology, including hidden visual elements and forensic-level features that are invisible to the naked eye.
"These features are integrated through highly advanced and costly printing technology, with the machinery involved valued at hundreds of millions of ringgit.
"The enhancements range from holographic, ultraviolet (UV) elements and even a specific security thread is used to stitch the passport booklets.
"Furthermore, every single page is unique; while the general design may appear uniform, detailed forensic analysis will show distinct differences on each page," he added.
Zakaria urged the public not to rush to immigration offices solely to obtain the new version if their current passports are still valid.
"I appeal to the public not to rush or feel pressured to change their passports immediately just to obtain the new features.
"If your current passport still has more than six months of validity, there is no need to renew it yet. You should only renew once the current document expires," he said.
He noted that the department had observed a slight dip in daily applications recently, suggesting that many citizens are waiting for the June 1 launch.
This has raised concerns about a potential surge in demand that could overwhelm system capacity.
The rollout will be conducted in stages, beginning June 1 at four selected branches – Immigration Headquarters in Putrajaya, the Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department Office on Jalan Duta,UTC Wangsa Maju and the Shah Alam Immigration Office.
Following the initial launch, the department expects to roll out the new passport to all remaining branches nationwide by the end of July.
Zakaria also clarified that passport application procedures remain unchanged. He added that the department is moving toward full integration with the National Integrated Immigration System (NIISe).
"Our systems are moving toward full NIISe implementation. This means that everything we are doing now, even while using existing systems, will eventually be fully integrated into the NIISe framework," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTD: Over 100 luxury vehicles still dodging road tax
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Road Transport Department (RTD) believes more than 100 luxury vehicles involved in various offences are still being driven on roads following the seizure of 42 vehicles under Op Khas Luxury 2.0.
Kelantan RTD director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said the department would continue tracking down the vehicles, most of which were found without valid Motor Vehicle Licences (road tax) and insurance protection.
He said the 42 luxury vehicles seized during the four-day operation were valued between RM150,000 and RM500,000 each.
"Overall, the estimated leakage in government revenue involving unpaid road tax for the 42 seized vehicles is about RM34,550 annually.
"The highest road tax recorded was RM6,612 per year, while the lowest was RM70 annually," he told reporters today.
Misuari said among the luxury vehicles seized were Porsche 911 GT3, Porsche Panamera V6, Jeep Wrangler, Toyota Alphard, Toyota Vellfire, BMW 528i Limousine, Audi TTS and Mercedes-Benz models.
"Based on our statistics, most of the seized luxury vehicles were found committing offences related to expired road tax and insurance, which indirectly contributed to government revenue leakages.
"The operation also uncovered luxury vehicle owners who only renewed their insurance coverage without renewing their road tax," he said.
He added that some vehicles were still being used on the road despite their road tax and insurance having expired since 2020.
"They gave excuses such as forgetting to renew the documents and some looked down on us, thinking no action would be taken against them as they are driving luxury cars," he said.
Misuari said the offenders comprised various groups, including businessmen and civil servants, with all vehicle owners identified as locals.
He added that enforcement patrols were conducted at various locations, particularly during peak hours, after office hours and at night when many vehicle owners were out at restaurants and coffee shops.
"Once they settle their summonses and renew the documents, RTD will proceed with the vehicle release process," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MHA to deepen collaboration with Media Prima on highway safety
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia Highway Authority (MHA) is strengthening its strategic cooperation with Media Prima Bhd to enhance highway user safety awareness and expand the dissemination of information related to facilities and traffic management.
Its director-general Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff said the collaboration would allow highway-related safety messages and information on facilities to reach a wider audience, particularly the estimated six million users across 34 highways under the MHA.
"With Media Prima's media strength, information, particularly on safety, can be channelled to our highway users.
"We have observed that, as of now, the accident rate on our highways is relatively high. So, with cooperation with Media Prima, we can work together to help reduce accident rates on our highways.
"At the same time, we also want to highlight the facilities available on the highways, as we are aware that rest and service areas and lay-bys are heavily used and are key points of focus for users," he said when met after a visit to Media Prima's headquarters at Balai Berita in Bangsar here.
During the visit, Hadzmir met Media Prima's top management and was received by group managing director Datuk Rafiq Razali.
Also present were Media Prima's News and Current Affairs division group managing editor Jasbant Singh, deputy group managing editor of News and Current Affairs and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Abd Karim, as well as other senior management.
Commenting on highway safety issues, particularly the implementation of Smart Lane, Hadzmir said there are currently 32 Smart Lane locations on highways nationwide, activated mainly during traffic congestion and festive seasons.
He said that while there were initial complaints regarding the use of the emergency lane as an additional lane, including claims that it made the road narrower and posed risks to motorcyclists, the situation has improved following various advocacy and awareness efforts.
"Smart Lane is one of the fastest and most practical solutions to increase road capacity without the need for new lane construction, which takes a long time and involves high costs," he said.
"Based on accident data obtained, Smart Lane implementation has also helped reduce accidents in the emergency lane as users are now more cautious and understand that the lane is shared with other vehicles," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EPF posts 51pct income jump to RM27.73bil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Employees Provident Fund (EPF)'s total investment income jumped 51 per cent to RM27.73 billion for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026) from RM18.31 billion a year ago.
The performance includes unrealised mark-to-market gains and losses on securities, consistent with previous years, primarily driven by foreign exchange rate fluctuations.
EPF chief executive officer Ahmad Zulqarnain Onn said the strong first-quarter income reflects a portfolio decision taken at the beginning of this year to realise gains ahead of anticipated market turbulence.
"Our portfolio managers front-loaded income that would otherwise have been spread across the full year.
"Members should not extrapolate this quarter's result, as it is unlikely to be repeated in subsequent quarters.
"The underlying portfolio continues to be managed for sustainable, long-term returns and not short-term peaks," he said in a statement today.
Ahmad Zulqarnain said the outlook ahead remains challenging, with elevated geopolitical risks, rising oil prices and renewed inflationary pressures posing meaningful headwinds to global markets.
"We entered this period of uncertainty in a position of strength because we acted early.
"Our priority now is capital preservation and disciplined deployment to ensure the adequacy and sustainability of retirement savings for our 18 million members over the long term," he added.
According to the EPF, equities income surged 88 per cent to RM20.34 billion in Q1 2026 from RM10.80 billion last year.
This was supported by broad-based gains across global markets in the early part of the quarter as improving investor sentiment lifted most major indices.
However, the momentum moderated in March, as escalating geopolitical tensions and heightened global uncertainty drove increased market volatility.
Meanwhile, fixed income instruments contributed RM6.76 billion, or 24 per cent of total investment income in Q1 2026.
The asset class, comprising Malaysian Government Securities and equivalents, as well as loans and bonds, continued to deliver stable and resilient returns, providing a key defensive buffer to the overall portfolio amid uncertain market conditions.
Real estate and infrastructure generated RM190 million in income in Q1 2026, while money market instruments contributed RM440 million, broadly in line with expected returns for these asset classes.
Total investment assets stood at RM1.44 trillion, with 36 per cent allocated to global markets.
International investments generated RM15.36 billion, or 55 per cent of total investment income for the quarter, reflecting the importance of geographic diversification in enhancing returns while managing portfolio risk.
Total investment income for conventional savings was RM22.63 billion, while Syariah accounts recorded RM5.1 billion.
The EPF added 220,925 new members in Q1 2026, bringing total membership to more than 18.3 million.
Active membership rose to 10.8 million, with the active-to-inactive ratio improving to 59:41. Active employers increased to 642,609 as at March 2026, following 19,956 new registrations during the quarter.
Total contributions grew 13.3 per cent to RM38 billion in Q1 2026 from RM33.54 billion in the same period a year earlier.
Voluntary contributions continued to gain traction, rising to RM8.83 billion, reflecting sustained member participation supported by EPF's digital platforms, which enable seamless and accessible contributions.
Meanwhile, the number of Malaysian formal sector members contributing above statutory rates through i-Topup increased 19.8 per cent to 232,305.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: After mayor's murder, Mexico battles to bring peace
Article:
THE brazen murder of a small-city mayor shocked Mexico and forced President Claudia Sheinbaum to step up an offensive against all-powerful drug cartels.
But even after a deployment totalling thousands troops and the killing of a top cartel capo, she has struggled to impose peace in restive Pacific coast states.
Residents of the state of Michoacan's second city Uruapan, have resigned themselves to live with violence.
"You can't be out on the streets too late anymore," 24-year-old student Natalia Miranda told AFP. "If you're assaulted, you don't make it out alive."
Uruapan, a city of more than 300,000 people, is the center of Mexico's multi-billion-dollar avocado industry. It's also a stronghold of several of the world's largest cocaine and fentanyl trafficking cartels.
In the town square, people chat amiably in the shadow of a memorial to slain cowboy-hat-wearing mayor Carlos Manzo.
The 40-year-old was shot multiple times during November's Day of the Dead festival, allegedly by a 17-year-old drug addict working for the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
After his death, protesters set fire to public buildings and clashed with police, resulting in over 100 injuries.
Uruapan has a long history of violence. In 2006, armed groups tossed five human heads onto a nightclub dance floor in the city.
In the 20 years since, there has been a cycle of intensifying violence.
"Michoacan was on the brink of becoming a failed state," governor Alfredo Ramirez Bedolla told AFP, recounting how farmers created self-defence militias just to survive.
But the situation reached a point of inflection with Manzo's murder, as much for residents as for Sheinbaum's government.
Around 20 people were arrested for Manzo's killing, including seven of his bodyguards.
The young gunman was killed by the mayor's security team at the scene.
"We can't forget what happened," said Manzo's widow, Grecia Quiroz, who has been the mayor of Uruapan since her husband's death.
She has no political experience but took office as a symbol of resistance and to demand that Sheinbaum tackle the cartels.
"This awoke not just Michoacan, but all of Mexico," she told AFP while surrounded by bodyguards to keep would-be assassins at bay.
"We cannot ignore or turn a blind eye to the issue of security," she said.
"It is time for society to come together, for us to demand from the government what must be demanded, for us to raise our voices, for us not to remain silent, and for fear to no longer paralyse us."
Following Manzo's death, President Sheinbaum vowed "zero impunity" and sent additional troops to Michoacan.
[[nid:1443361]]
She also moved against Nemesio Oseguera, a Michoacan native and notorious leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
"El Mencho", as he was better known, was captured in February in Jalisco state in a military sweep and died of his injuries on route to the hospital.
The cartel responded nationwide by setting up highway blockades and burning buildings in 20 of Mexico's 32 states.
Over 60 people died, the majority in neighbouring Jalisco.
The authorities escalated further, arresting Audias Flores, El Mencho's presumed successor, as well as his top accountant and other lieutenants.
Since Sheinbaum came to office in late 2024, more than 52,000 people have been detained for suspected cartel ties.
She has shifted away from the "hugs not bullets" strategy of her predecessor and leftist political mentor, Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador.
There has been a renewed focus on strengthening the National Guard, boosting intelligence, and targeted troop deployments.
But still, polls show 60 per cent of Mexicans feel unsafe, and Mexico's first female president is under intense pressure from Washington.
President Donald Trump has threatened to strike targets south of the border if Mexican authorities do not act.
The US government has also trained its sights on another target: "narco politicians."
US federal prosecutors indicted the governor of neighbouring Sinaloa state, who is a member of Sheinbaum's ruling Morena party.
So far, Mexican authorities have refused to arrest him, claiming there is a lack of evidence.
Today, camouflaged National Guard troops patrol Uruapan with rifles nestled, or cruise the streets in pickup trucks mounted with machine guns.
"You might say to yourself, 'it's better to stay in my house to stay safe,'" said 50-year-old resident Teresa Silva.
Silva, a housewife, rests on a bench in the plaza, a few meters from a military checkpoint.
"It's a little bit quieter," she says.
The violence means she leaves her house less, but she does not have any other option than living with fear.
"The only thing we can do is keep living," she said.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Blind community set to benefit from flexible digital learning initiative
Article:
OUM president Professor Datuk Dr Ahmad Izanee Awang (second left) and MAB deputy president Datin Fauziah Ramly (second right) at the MoU signing ceremony between OUM and MAB at the MAB Complex in Brickfields. – NSTP/Aziah Azmee
KUALA LUMPUR: Open University Malaysia (OUM) and the Malaysian Association for the Blind (MAB) today took a step towards improving higher education access for the visually impaired community through flexible digital learning.
The memorandum of understanding (MoU), signed at the MAB Complex in Brickfields, will allow MAB staff and trainees to pursue studies from certificate to Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) level.
MAB deputy president Datin Fauziah Ramly said the collaboration was aimed at ensuring persons with visual impairment were not left behind in education and workforce development.
"We believe visual impairment is not a barrier to success. With the right opportunities and access, every individual has the potential to contribute to society and national development," she said.
She said challenges remained in areas such as access to education, assistive technology and employment opportunities, making collaborations between educational institutions and community organisations increasingly important.
Fauziah added that the partnership would also involve professional training, research, internships and industry exposure for students.
"OUM's flexible and digital learning model can help visually impaired learners overcome difficulties in travelling to campuses and attending physical classes," she said.
OUM president and vice-chancellor Datuk Professor Dr Ahmad Izanee Awang said technology had made higher education more accessible to the visually impaired community.
"We utilise digital learning materials and flexible online classes, allowing students to learn anytime and anywhere," he said.
Ahmad said the university was prepared to improve its digital support and learning delivery based on the needs of visually impaired students.
"OUM already employs several visually impaired staff members in various departments, including student affairs and its call centre," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saifuddin: Immigration Department facing headwinds from sophisticated syndicates
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said increasingly sophisticated syndicates are exploiting technological loopholes and shifting routes, placing growing pressure on the country's Immigration Department.
"These syndicates no longer rely on conventional methods. They utilise manipulated passports, forged identities and document-swapping at transit airports to circumvent enforcement," he said during the Immigration Department's 2025 Excellence Service Awards ceremony here today.
"Irregular migration routes have evolved into a dynamic network that shifts in response to enforcement pressures and emerging opportunities," he added.
"As an open trading nation and a primary mobility hub, Malaysia sits at the heart of these strategic regional routes. We are neither isolated nor immune to these pressures," he said.
The country's vast and geographically complex land and maritime borders remain vulnerable to exploitation by syndicates smuggling undocumented workers.
Traffickers have become more sophisticated, using
layered routes
through transit hubs such as Singapore or Thailand before moving migrants into the country via illegal jetties.
Citing data from the International Organisation for Migration (IOM), the minister said the Asia-Pacific is currently the world's most active migration corridor.
Between January and October 2025, more than 1.6 million Myanmar nationals entered Thailand via land routes, 51 per cent of whom were undocumented.
During the same period, about 5,000 refugees crossed the Bay of Bengal and the Andaman Sea, facilitated by organised smuggling networks.
Saifuddin warned that transnational crime, human trafficking and migrant smuggling flourish when global and regional economies face instability.
"These entities capitalise on global volatility and human vulnerability to maximise their illicit profits. When livelihoods become unstable, people move in search of sanctuary and employment, and syndicates are quick to exploit that desperation," he said.
Despite these pressures, the department managed more than 115 million movements at the country's entry points in 2025, involving 43 million international visitors.
The department also oversees 2.4 million long-term pass holders, including foreign workers, international students and expatriates.
Saifuddin praised the efficiency of the Immigration Department, which generated RM5.2 billion in revenue in 2025, a five-fold return on its RM1.1 billion operating budget.
He said the performance was achieved despite regional security challenges and a high volume of international travellers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Concession growth, insulin expansion to drive Pharmaniaga's earnings outlook
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pharmaniaga Bhd expects continued growth in its concession business after the government raised the allocation under the system to RM2.1 billion this year from RM2 billion in 2025.
The company is also expanding its insulin business with localised production set to begin in the third quarter of 2026.
Pharmaniaga managing director Datuk Zulkifli Jafar said actual orders under the concession system are expected to exceed RM2.2 billion this year as more products are added to the Approved Products Purchase List (APPL).
He said Pharmaniaga is also strengthening its insulin operations.
The company is currently able to produce up to 25 million insulin cartridges annually under a two-shift operation, with capacity expandable under a three-shift system.
"We are prioritising domestic supply to government healthcare facilities first before exploring export opportunities," he told reporters after the company's annual general meeting (AGM) and extraordinary general meeting (EGM) today.
The meetings also saw shareholders overwhelmingly approving all resolutions tabled.
This includes a proposed five-to-one share consolidation exercise aimed at strengthening the company's capital market positioning following its exit from Practice Note 17 (PN17) status.
Zulkifli said nearly 99 per cent of shareholders voted in favour of the proposal, reflecting investor confidence in the pharmaceutical company's recovery and long-term strategy.
Under the exercise, every five existing shares will be consolidated into one share, reducing its issued shares from 6.56 billion to about 1.31 billion shares under the minimum scenario and 1.32 billion shares under the maximum scenario.
He stressed that the exercise would not materially affect Pharmaniaga's market capitalisation or shareholders' total investment value, apart from minor adjustments.
Pharmaniaga exited PN17 status in March 2026 after completing its regularisation plan.
The company posted a net profit of RM32.2 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, compared with RM30.2 million a year earlier, marking its ninth consecutive profitable quarter.
Zulkifli said the performance was driven by stronger contributions from its core logistics and distribution operations as well as pharmaceutical manufacturing.
He added that Pharmaniaga continues to pursue cost optimisation through digitalisation and automation initiatives to improve operational efficiency.
It is currently testing fully automated warehouse operations at its new Penang facility as part of efforts to reduce costs to a target of five per cent while improving productivity.
Pharmaniaga also signalled plans to resume quarterly dividend payments after recently declaring its first dividend in more than two years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to retable bill limiting PM's tenure in Dewan Rakyat next month
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government will retable a constitutional amendment to limit a prime minister's tenure to 10 years during the upcoming parliamentary session in June, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said.
She said the cabinet has finalised discussions on the matter, adding that the bill would require a two-thirds majority in Parliament as it involves a constitutional amendment.
"In the June session, we will be retabling the 10-year term limit for the prime minister. This has been discussed in the cabinet over the past few months, and we are bringing it back to Parliament to seek the required two-thirds majority, as it involves a constitutional amendment," she said.
Azalina told this to reporters at the Excellent Service Award 2025 for the Law and Institutional Reform Cluster in Putrajaya today.
She said she hoped the June sitting would see progress on two key reforms — the term limit for the nation's top office and the long-awaited separation of the roles of the attorney-general and the public prosecutor.
Addressing concerns over opposition backing, given that constitutional amendments require a two-thirds majority, Azalina urged all members of parliament to view the move as a step toward better governance.
"The most important thing is for MPs to understand that this is about limiting power, not expanding it.
"From the government's perspective, we see this as something positive for the country and the people. Several political parties within the government have already included this in their manifestos, and I hope the opposition will see its benefits as well," she said.
Asked whether the government would conduct further engagement sessions with the opposition ahead of the tabling, Azalina said the focus remains on bringing the bill back to the floor for a decision.
"We will simply proceed with retabling it," she said.
On another matter, Azalina said there was significant progress in the establishment of the Anti-Bullying Tribunal, confirming that its chairman, Nurul Husna Awang, commenced her duties yesterday.
"On June 16, we will officially launch the Anti-Bullying Tribunal. This will include both a physical court and an online platform," she said.
The tribunal is expected to provide a speedier, quasi-judicial alternative for victims of bullying, particularly those under the age of 18, to seek redress and compensation.
On the Virtual Private Network (VPN) Bill, Azalina said no major overhauls are planned for the current draft, saying that the government would only be adding community-related improvements to the regulations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Refugee policy proposal to be presented to cabinet by mid-June
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Home Ministry is set to present a proposal to the cabinet on the nation's new refugee management framework by mid-June.
Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the proposal, which is currently being finalised, follows a significant "game-changing" pilot project currently underway at the Juru Immigration Depot.
"Coincidentally, I visited the Juru Immigration Depot yesterday. A special project of ours is currently underway there, a major project that has the potential to be a game changer for a significant national issue, namely comprehensive refugee management.
"The idea is at an early stage and was mooted by the Immigration director-general to me.
"But now we have an additional task, to prepare the report for the cabinet in the second week of June as a Cabinet note at the request of the Prime Minister," he said at the Immigration Department's 2026 Outstanding Service Awards, today.
The proposal has already gained traction at the highest levels of government after he presented the idea at the National Economic Council meeting.
The move comes as the government ramps up its Refugee Registration Document (DPP) program designed to track and manage the country's refugee population more systematically.
Yesterday, Saifuddin said that
refugees and asylum seekers registered under the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) program
will not be allowed to permanently settle in Malaysia, even if they are matched with employers.
The program will ultimately lead to only three possible outcomes: repatriation, third-country resettlement, or deportation for legal offenders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Spanish ex-PM Zapatero under investigation for influence peddling: Court
Article:
MADRID: Spain's former Socialist Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero has been placed under investigation for influence peddling and related crimes, a court said Tuesday.
Zapatero, who served as prime minister from 2004 to 2011, has been summoned to testify on June 2, Spain's top criminal court said in a statement.
The case centres on the bailout of Venezuela-linked airline Plus Ultra during the Covid-19 pandemic – years after Zapatero had left office.
The airline received a 53 million euro (US$62 million) public loan in March 2021 from a government fund set up to support strategic companies hit by the crisis.
The rescue of Plus Ultra sparked controversy in Spain, with opposition parties questioning the airline's financial situation and its links to Venezuela.
This is the first time that a former Spanish prime minister has been placed under formal investigation since the country returned to democracy following the death of longtime dictator Francisco Franco in 1975.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ten Gaza-bound aid vessels in final stretch to destination, arrival expected in nine hours
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ten Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 vessels, carrying four Malaysians, are continuing their voyage to Gaza and are expected to arrive within nine hours.
Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said the vessels were in Red Zone 2, about 100 nautical miles from the Gaza coast.
"Based on the estimated distance to Gaza and a travelling speed of about 15km/h, or around eight knots, we expect them to arrive in another nine hours. It will be a very tense period," he said at a press conference here today.
"These final hours mark the last stretch for these young Malaysians, whom we should be proud of for remaining steadfast and continuing their mission without turning back," he added.
Sani Araby said the vessels still en route to Gaza were Alcyone, Andros, Cabo Blanco, Don Juan, Elengi, Girolama, Kasr-i-Sadabad, Lina, Sirius and Zefiro.
He confirmed that the four Malaysians involved in the mission remained safe, identifying them as Hareez and Chin aboard Alcyone, Syed Amirul on Don Juan, and Noor Thanisah on Zefiro.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia riding out oil shock from strength: Standard Chartered
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is entering the current oil crisis from a position of strength despite heightened uncertainty surrounding global growth, according to Standard Chartered.
The bank said the country's resilient economic performance, firm private investment and improving external position provide a solid buffer against ongoing geopolitical and commodity market volatility.
Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) expanded 5.4 per cent year-on-year in the first quarter (Q1) of 2026, supported mainly by strong services exports and private investment.
"While sequential growth was flat, we highlight that GDP had risen consecutively for the previous 12 quarters. Services exports and private investment were strong in the Q1," Standard Chartered said in a note.
The bank maintained its 2026 GDP growth forecast at 4.5 per cent.
At the same time, it said Bank Negara Malaysia's focus appeared to be shifting towards inflation management following its May monetary policy meeting.
Standard Chartered pointed to subtle changes in the central bank's policy statement, including the characterisation of the monetary policy stance as "appropriate and consistent" instead of the earlier "appropriate and supportive".
According to the firm, the revised wording suggests Bank Negara may be moving away from a neutral stance, increasing the likelihood of an interest rate hike should inflationary pressures broaden further.
Meanwhile, Malaysia's balance of payments (BoP) position remained strong in the first quarter, recording a surplus equivalent to 3.2 per cent of GDP.
Most components posted favourable net inflows, while the services balance registered a third consecutive quarterly surplus, aided by strong tourism activity and rising infocomm services receipts.
Standard Chartered noted that the latest trend marked a turnaround after 14 years of persistent services deficits.
The bank also highlighted that Malaysian residents repatriated offshore equity holdings worth 1.1 per cent of GDP during the quarter, marking the first such inflow in two years.
Foreign investors also continued purchasing domestic equities and debt securities, with combined inflows amounting to 1.5 per cent of GDP.
Excluding errors and omissions outflows, the research house estimated Malaysia's Q1 BoP surplus would have stood at about eight per cent of GDP.
Standard Chartered said it remains constructive on the outlook for the ringgit, supported by the country's strengthening external position.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Male sexual harassment reports rising, says Nancy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sexual harassment cases involving male victims have recorded a significant rise, with more than 1,000 reports received through the complaints counter at the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry since 2024, says Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri.
The minister said a similar upward trend was also reflected in police statistics, indicating that advocacy programmes by the ministry had encouraged more victims to come forward and lodge reports.
Nancy said physical sexual harassment cases reported by male victims included unwanted physical contact involving certain parts of the body.
"For example, there was an incident at a shopping complex where a male victim was suddenly approached and held from behind by a male salesperson while he was about to enter the fitting room," she told reporters after officiating the Zero Tolerance of Violence: Say No to Sexual Harassment programme today.
She said that based on complaints received by the Tribunal for Anti-Sexual Harassment (TAGS), three cases involving male victims had been recorded, with some already decided in favour of the complainants.
On the alleged sexual harassment case involving a district police chief in Sarawak, Nancy said the matter was still under police investigation.
"The ministry commends the courage of the officer involved for coming forward to report the case, and will continue to monitor its development closely," she said.
Commenting on sexual harassment cases involving children at home, Nancy urged family members to take all complaints seriously, especially when they involved close relatives.
"We need to always listen to what they say, as children sometimes feel very afraid to talk to their mothers and siblings for fear of being accused of making up stories.
"Through the advocacy programmes we run, children are now starting to become aware of their rights. In fact, some are seeking alternative channels to lodge complaints, including contacting Talian Kasih 15999 or using modern technology such as artificial intelligence (AI) and ChatGPT to seek help," she said.
Earlier in her speech, Nancy said that as of April 26, a total of 92 complaints had been filed with TAGS, with 69 successfully resolved, demonstrating the tribunal's effectiveness in expediting justice for victims.
She said police statistics showed a worrying upward trend, with sexual harassment cases rising from 477 in 2022 to 1,038 in 2025, reflecting the seriousness of the issue and the need for firmer action and greater public awareness.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ebola deaths in eastern Congo rise to 131 as outbreak spreads
Article:
BUNIA: Twenty-six more suspected Ebola deaths were recorded in 24 hours in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, authorities said on Tuesday, and the head of the World Health Organisation expressed deep concern about the outbreak's spread.
The new deaths bring to 131 the fatalities associated with the outbreak in eastern DRC.
There have been 516 suspected cases and 33 confirmed cases in Congo, according to a daily bulletin published by health authorities, and two confirmed cases in neighbouring Uganda.
WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus declared the outbreak of the rare Bundibugyo strain of the virus a public health emergency of international concern on Saturday.
It has alarmed experts because it was able to spread for weeks undetected across a densely populated part of Congo.
Butembo, a city of hundreds of thousands of people in Congo's North Kivu province, recorded its first two confirmed cases on Monday, Jean-Jacques Muyembe, director of Congo's National ​Institute for Biomedical Research (INRB), told Reuters.
Ebola spreads through direct contact with bodily fluids from infected people or animals and causes symptoms that can include high fever, vomiting and internal ⁠and external bleeding.
According to the WHO, the average fatality rate from Ebola is around 50 per cent, varying from 25 to 90 per cent in past ⁠outbreaks.
"I'm deeply concerned about the scale and speed of the epidemic," Tedros told members of the World Health Assembly in Geneva on Tuesday, citing the number of cases being reported in urban areas and among healthcare workers.
One American tested positive for Ebola as part of their work in Congo, the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said on Monday.
The individual, identified as Dr Peter Stafford by his Christian mission organisation, and six other Americans who were exposed to the virus are being moved to Germany for care and monitoring, the CDC said.
The US State Department said it had mobilised an initial US$13 million in foreign assistance for immediate response efforts to the outbreak.
Unlike with the more common Zaire strain of Ebola, there is no approved virus-specific ​therapeutics or vaccine for the Bundibugyo strain.
The US is working to develop a monoclonal antibody therapy as a potential treatment, CDC said on Monday.
A panel of experts led by the WHO will also meet on Tuesday to discuss if there are any vaccine options to help tackle the outbreak.
The US officially left the WHO in January in a move President Donald Trump said was motivated by the organisation's poor management of the Covid-19 pandemic.
An outbreak of the Zaire strain from 2018-2020 in Ituri and North Kivu provinces was the second deadliest on record, killing nearly 2,300 people.
The international response then was complicated by widespread armed violence in eastern Congo that continues today.
One Ebola case has been confirmed in North Kivu province's capital, ​Goma, which was seized by M23 rebels last year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nestle partners SWCorp to ramp up plastic recycling in Kedah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Nestle Malaysia has partnered with SWCorp to strengthen the plastic waste recycling ecosystem in Kedah.
The collaboration will support the expansion of Project SAVE (Segregate, Avoid, Value, Educate), Nestle Malaysia's flagship voluntary recycling initiative, through community-led recycling efforts in Kulim.
The partnership was highlighted during the Karnival Kitar community event, reflecting the company's commitment towards promoting a more circular and waste-free future through voluntary Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) programmes.
Project SAVE, launched in 2020, is Nestle Malaysia's flagship voluntary recycling programme and the country's largest voluntary EPR initiative.
The programme supports household separation-at-source (SAS) through weekly door-to-door collection of dry mixed recyclables in collaboration with municipal councils, waste management concessionaires and private recyclers.
Nestle Malaysia said the programme has now expanded nationwide, reaching about 260,000 households across nine cities.
In 2025 alone, Project SAVE diverted an estimated 15,000 tonnes of solid waste from landfills and oceans, including 10,000 tonnes of plastic waste.
The initiative was expanded to Kulim this year through a pilot collaboration involving SWCorp, E-Idaman and ESH Resource.
Since its introduction in Kulim, the pilot project has collected approximately 100,000kg of recyclables, including around 28,000kg of plastics, through its dedicated weekly collection service.
Chief executive officer Juan Aranols said effective recycling requires both strong partnerships and active participation from households.
"The progress we have seen shows that effective recycling starts with strong partnerships and consistent systems on the ground. But systems alone are not enough — real change starts at home," he said.
He added that Nestle Malaysia's voluntary EPR approach reflects the company's commitment to supporting a more circular and waste-free future.
SWCorp chief executive officer Khalid Mohammed said the partnership highlights the importance of a structured collection system in encouraging effective community recycling.
"Strong coordination among operational partners, local authorities and residents is critical to ensuring efficiency and sustaining participation over time," he said.
The MoU exchange was witnessed by Tunku Temenggong Kedah Tan Sri Tunku Puteri Intan Safinaz Sultan Abdul Halim.
Held at SMK Taman Selasih, Karnival Kitar brought together residents, schools, local authorities and community leaders through educational roadshows, interactive booths and awareness activities aimed at promoting recycling practices and recognising community contributions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mango clothing founder's son arrested in Spain over tycoon's death
Article:
BARCELONA: Spanish police said Tuesday they have arrested the eldest son of Mango clothing empire founder Isak Andic over his father's death during a hiking trip in December 2024.
Jonathan Andic, who was alone with his 71-year-old father when the retail magnate plunged to his death in the Montserrat mountains near Barcelona, was taken into custody, Catalan regional police said, confirming a report in the daily La Vanguardia newspaper.
Authorities said at the time that he fell from a height near the Salnitre caves in Collbato, an area marked by steep drops and ravines.
Investigators initially treated the death as an accident, with early findings suggesting Isak may have slipped. A judge closed the case in January 2025 after finding no evidence of criminal wrongdoing.
However, investigators with Catalonia's regional police force, the Mossos d'Esquadra, along with prosecutors and the court, reopened the investigation in October 2025 after citing inconsistencies in Jonathan Andic's testimony, according to the reports.
Jonathan Andic has denied any responsibility for his father's death and has maintained that the fall was accidental.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor media group lodges police report over alleged security interference at convention [WATCH]
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Kelab Media Johor (KMJ) has lodged a police report over an alleged incident involving aggressive conduct by a political party's security personnel against media practitioners covering a convention here on Sunday.
In a statement today, the club said the incident occurred during coverage of a political gathering here on Sunday, where several journalists from Johor and Kuala Lumpur were allegedly obstructed while attempting to obtain reactions from party leaders.
The club said security personnel assigned to control crowd movement and escort dignitaries had allegedly acted excessively, resulting in pushing, verbal altercations and disruption to media duties.
KMJ said the situation had caused discomfort and raised safety concerns among journalists carrying out their professional duties.
"Such an incident should not have happened, especially after assurances were given by organisers that proper access and space would be provided to facilitate media operations," the statement said.
The club added that it viewed media practitioners as strategic partners in disseminating information to the public and said journalists should be accorded professional cooperation by organisers and security teams.
KMJ said members had lodged a police report at the Johor Baru Sentral police station today to ensure similar incidents do not recur and to safeguard the welfare and rights of media personnel.
It also urged organisers and relevant parties to provide an immediate explanation over the alleged incident.
Meanwhile, the Pakatan Harapan convention secretariat said it was conducting an internal investigation into the incident, including gathering feedback from security personnel, programme organisers and other relevant parties.
It added that it would cooperate with authorities if required and improve media management and security coordination at future events.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New, harder to forge Malaysian passport rolling out June 1, public reminded not to use AI photos in applications
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 19 — The Immigration Department will begin issuing a new Malaysian passport with 94 security features from June 1, further strengthening the security of the nation’s identification document.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said that in the initial phase, the issuance of the new passport will involve four offices: the Immigration headquarters in Putrajaya; the Kuala Lumpur Passport Office in Jalan Duta; the Urban Transformation Centre (UTC) Wangsa Maju; and the Shah Alam Immigration Office.
He said the rollout of the new passport will be implemented in stages, with full nationwide issuance expected by July.
“Previously, our passports had 49 security features, but the new version has nearly doubled that number to 94.
“This is aimed at further enhancing security features so that our passports are more secure and more difficult to forge,” he said at a press conference after the 2025 Immigration Department Excellent Service Awards ceremony, which was officiated by Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail here today.
Commenting on the new security enhancements, Zakaria said among the improvements in the new passport are enhanced holograms, ultraviolet (UV) printing, hidden visual elements, and several specialised forensic features that are difficult to forge.
“Even the thread used to bind the passport booklet has its own security features, and each page is designed differently,” he said.
Zakaria also advised the public not to rush to replace their passports, noting that existing passports with more than six months’ validity remain usable and do not need to be replaced.
“Existing passports remain valid as long as they are within their validity period. The public is advised not to rush to obtain the new passport simply because of its new features.
“In fact, if the passport is still valid for more than six months, there is no need to replace it now. It can be renewed after it expires,” he said.
He added that applications for the new Malaysian passport can also be made online, although this depends on the capacity of the respective passport offices.
He also reminded the public applying online to use genuine photographs and not images altered using artificial intelligence (AI).
“I would like to stress that passport applicants should not use photos generated or modified using AI.
“We have detected several cases where applicants wanted better-looking photos - for example, brightening darker images or enhancing their appearance - and used AI,” he said, adding that Immigration officers have the expertise to detect AI-generated images.
Earlier this year, Saifuddin Nasution said Malaysia would introduce a new-look passport and MyKad within six months as part of efforts to strengthen the security of national identification documents.
He said the introduction of the new documents marks a significant milestone, particularly as the Malaysian passport is currently ranked among the most powerful in the world.
The Malaysian passport ranked third globally in 2025, placing it among the world’s most powerful passports. — Bernama

Title: Johor Media Club regrets incident involving media during Pakatan convention, police report filed
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 19 — The Johor Media Club (KMJ) has lodged a police report over the alleged aggressive conduct by security personnel against media practitioners covering the 2026 Pakatan Harapan (PH) Convention here on Sunday.
KMJ said the report lodged at the Johor Bahru Sentral police station earlier was a proactive step following to the alleged incident involving journalists from Johor and Kuala Lumpur.
The club said the security personnel were said to have obstructed media practitioners who were present at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here to cover the political convention.
“The situation involved physical and verbal altercations while the media practitioners were carrying out their tasks.
“The uncontrolled situation obstructed the media personnel’s work and also their safety while carrying out their professional responsibilities,” read a statement issued by KMJ today.
The statement added the situation had raised safety concerns among media practitioners who were carrying out their professional duties.
“The incident should not have happened, especially after assurances were earlier given by the organisers that proper access would be provided to facilitate media operations.
“In this regard, KMJ has taken proactive steps by filing a police report to ensure that such matters do not recur, thus protecting the welfare and rights of media personnel in carrying out their duties,” added the statement.
Following that, national media watchdog movement Gerakan Media Merdeka (Geramm) also expressed its concern over the incident.
On Sunday, PH’s top leadership attended the national convention here. The event was attended by Prime Minster Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and several senior ministers.

Title: Home Ministry to submit Bidor refugee detention centre report to Cabinet in June, says Saifuddin Nasution
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 19 — The Home Ministry (KDN) will present a report on the implementation of the Refugee and Asylum Seeker Special Detention Centre (PPKPPS) in Bidor, Perak, to the Cabinet next month.
Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the report would be tabled in line with a directive from Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during today’s National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting.
He said he had also asked KDN deputy secretary-general (Security) Datuk Abdul Halim Abdul Rahman to prepare an urgent Cabinet note on the matter.
“Next month, in the second week of June, we will submit it as a Cabinet note to our fellow ministers at the Prime Minister’s request.
“The KDN will have the opportunity to provide detailed updates on this special project, which is being implemented in line with responsibilities assigned by the National Security Council under comprehensive refugee management,” he said at the Immigration Department’s Excellent Service Awards 2025 ceremony today.
Saifuddin said the Bidor PPKPPS would be a game changer in Malaysia’s efforts to establish a more comprehensive refugee management system.
“This major project will determine success and has the potential to be a game changer in the country’s comprehensive refugee management agenda,” he said.
The KDN has previously taken proactive steps to strengthen policies on foreign nationals, including refugees and asylum seekers, through the establishment of the PPKPPS facility in Bidor.
The modern facility will function not only as a screening and documentation centre, but also as a national reference hub for security screening, biometric verification, documentation, and status assessment. — Bernama

Title: Former CM Salleh Said Keruak appointed as Sabah chief inspector of projects
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — Former chief minister Datuk Seri Salleh Said Keruak has been appointed as the Sabah Chief Inspector of Projects.
According to a statement from the Chief Minister’s Department, Salleh received his appointment letter from Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Haji Noor at Menara Kinabalu here, today.
In the statement, expressing his appreciation fo the Chief Minister, Salleh pledged to carry out the responsibility with full commitment, transparency and integrity.
Salleh, 68, is also the former Member of Parliament for Kota Belud and former Usukan Assemblymen.
He was also the Communications and Multimedia Minister from 2015 to 2018. — Bernama

Title: Rapid Rail identifies damaged train component as cause of yesterday’s peak-hour Kelana Jaya LRT disruption
Article:
